task_completion.description: Create a surface with at least 10,000 faces
task_completion.visual_evidence: A highly subdivided plane or grid is visible in the 3D viewport (e.g., dense wireframe in Edit Mode, or a Subdivision Surface modifier with high levels applied in the modifier stack).

task_completion.description: Apply procedural displacement to generate mountainous landforms
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Displace modifier is active on the surface. The Texture Properties panel shows a procedural texture type selected (Clouds, Musgrave, or Voronoi), and the mesh in the viewport is deformed into 3D mountainous shapes.
In the Displace modifier settings within the Modifier Properties panel, the 'Strength' value is set to a number between 1.0 and 10.0.

task_completion.description: Apply a natural green or brown material with roughness > 0.5
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Material Properties panel shows a material assigned to the terrain. The Base Color is set to a shade of green or brown (not default grey), and the Roughness value is greater than 0.5.

task_completion.description: Position the camera for a high-angle aerial view (5-30m height)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera object is selected, and the Transform panel (N-panel) or Object Properties shows its Z Location between 5m and 30m. The camera is angled downwards to capture the terrain.

task_completion.description: Render the scene and save the project
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender window title bar displays 'terrain_scene.blend' indicating the project was saved. A rendered image of the terrain is visible in the Image Editor or Render window, or a file browser shows 'terrain_render.png' being saved.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with Blender's 3D viewport, modifier panels, and material settings, rather than just writing a Python script in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: The rendered image matches the 3D viewport scene
integrity.visual_evidence: The final render shown in the Image Editor visually matches the terrain shape, camera angle, and material colors seen in the 3D viewport.

integrity.description: Files are saved to the correct requested directories
integrity.visual_evidence: Save dialogs, file browsers, or the Blender title bar explicitly show the path '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' being used for both the .blend and .png files.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The ground plane must be highly subdivided (at least 10,201 vertices, e.g., 100x100 cuts). Displacement strength must be strictly between 1.0 and 10.0. Camera height (Z-axis location) must be between 5.0 and 30.0 meters. The material base color must visibly differ from Blender's default grey (RGB 0.8, 0.8, 0.8) and be green or brown, with a roughness value >= 0.5. Expected save paths are /home/ga/BlenderProjects/terrain_scene.blend and /home/ga/BlenderProjects/terrain_render.png.
